details: Referenced as "CORAL" in KinAce aggregator but no independent homepage or clear documentation found. May be a component or alternate name for another resource.
Only referenced in Clinical Knowledge Graph (CKG) as a data source. No independent website or documentation accessible. Domain does not resolve.
Referenced in EpiGraphDB but no independent website found. Searched for PRS Atlas and PGS Catalog but found no matching independent resource. Domain does not resolve.
Richards Effector Gene List - GitHub wiki page exists but lacks sufficient detail about data products, publications behind paywall. Homepage points to lab website without specific REGL information.
Community Assessment of Community Annotation with Ontologies - Homepage URL (http://gowiki.tamu.edu/wiki/index.php/Category:CACAO) cannot be resolved or accessed.
Only referenced as a source in AlzKB knowledge graph. No independent website, documentation, or other references found. Domains do not resolve.
Only referenced as a source in UBKG knowledge graph. No independent website or clear documentation found. Not in OBO Foundry. Possible ontology or database acronym but cannot be verified.
No accessible information found. No PubMed results, no homepage, no database entries or references. May be an internal identifier or inactive resource.

KG-IDG graph download is offline during the KG-Hub reorganization. The kg-hub.berkeleybop.io host now serves a "site is being reorganized" notice listing kg-idg among projects that "will soon have new homes" (no replacement URL yet). All candidates 404: kg-hub.berkeleybop.io/kg-idg/{,current/}{kg-idg,KG-IDG}.tar.gz, kghub.io/kg-idg/, dated release dirs, S3 bucket listings, and GitHub releases (none). README and Sphinx docs both point to the now-dead kg-hub.berkeleybop.io/kg-idg/ location.

KG-COVID-19 graph download is offline after the KG-Hub reorganization. Old product_url https://kghub.io/kg-covid-19/ 404s. README.rst and Sphinx docs (kghub.org/kg-covid-19/getting_started.html) both point to kg-hub.berkeleybop.io/kg-covid-19/current/kg-covid-19.tar.gz (and .nt.gz), which now 404. kghub.org/MANIFEST.yaml still lists dated releases (latest 20220902) under kg-hub.berkeleybop.io/kg-covid-19/<date>/kg-covid-19.tar.gz, but every dated and current artifact 404s on kg-hub.berkeleybop.io and kghub.io, and the kg-hub-public-data S3 bucket returns 403 AccessDenied (objects no longer public; bucket listing denied). Sibling projects (e.g. kg-microbe) 404/403 identically, confirming a host-wide migration rather than deletion of this graph specifically. No replacement public download URL advertised anywhere; no GitHub releases. Last build 20220902.

Open Targets retired its REST API in favour of GraphQL. Raw endpoint https://api.platform.opentargets.org/api/v4/rest returns 404 (also api/v4/rest/). The GraphQL endpoint https://api.platform.opentargets.org/api/v4/graphql is live (200 on POST, 400 on GET as expected for GraphQL). The opentargets.api.rest product already points to live GraphQL API docs (https://platform-docs.opentargets.org/data-access/graphql-api, 200) and its description notes the REST retirement; product left in place, not removed.

CTD2 Dashboard product (ctd2.dashboard) product_url https://ctd2-dashboard.nci.nih.gov/ is dead: the host has no DNS record (Could not resolve host) as of 2026-06-27, while a known-good control (www.cancer.gov) returns 200, so this is a real decommission, not a network issue. Candidate hosts probed: ctd2-dashboard.nci.nih.gov (NO DNS), ctd2.ccr.cancer.gov (NO DNS), ocg.cancer.gov (NO DNS), ctd2-data.nci.nih.gov (DNS resolves, 301 -> https://studycatalog.cancer.gov), ctd2.nci.nih.gov and ctd2-dashboard.nci.nih.gov/dashboard/ (NO DNS). The CBIIT/nci-ctd2-dashboard GitHub repo (last activity 2022, release v5.6_20221012) still references only the now-dead http://ctd2-dashboard.nci.nih.gov/. Current NCI CTD2 pages (data-portal, using-ctd2-data, dashboard blog/help) no longer link to any live dashboard app; CTD2 data has been consolidated into the Index of NCI Studies / Study Catalog at studycatalog.cancer.gov, already represented by the ctd2.data-portal product (https://www.cancer.gov/ccg/research/functional-genomics/ctd2/data-portal, 200). No live replacement for the standalone dashboard application found. Product retained per skill (not removed); warning added to ctd2.dashboard on owner page and per-product stub; homepage_url unaffected (cancer.gov ctd2 page returns 200).

KG-Alzheimers graph download offline during KG-Hub migration. Old product_url https://kg-hub.berkeleybop.io/kg-alzheimers/current/kg-alzheimers.tar.gz 404s, as do kghub.org/kg-alzheimers/current/kg-alzheimers.tar.gz and the kg-hub.berkeleybop.io/kg-alzheimers/ listing (serves a "KG-Hub Error Page"). The GitHub repo Knowledge-Graph-Hub/kg-alzheimers has zero releases (API returns []) and zero tags, so unlike sibling kg-phenio there is no GitHub releases mirror to recover from. kghub.org/kg-alzheimers/index.html docs page still loads (200) but advertises no live download. No replacement public download URL found; product left in place per skill.

DKK Expression Browser at http://expression.darkkinome.org/ returns HTTP 500 (hosted Shiny app crashed/retired); main darkkinome.org still links to it but the subdomain is down. Repointed darkkinasekb.expression product_url to live GitHub repo https://github.com/IDG-Kinase/kinase_expression (HTTP 200) which holds the expression data and browser code. Main homepage_url https://darkkinome.org is live (200).

dbSNO download product (dbsno.downloads) product_url https://biomics.lab.nycu.edu.tw/dbSNO/download.php returns HTTP 500 consistently (3/3 retries) as of 2026-06-27. The page serves only a 4095-byte partial HTML shell (header+navbar) and the PHP errors out before emitting any download links or data-file URLs, so no usable replacement artifact URL exists. The rest of the dbSNO 3.0 site is live: https://biomics.lab.nycu.edu.tw/dbSNO/ (200), index.php (200), statistics.php (200). The legacy host https://csb.cse.yzu.edu.tw/dbSNO/ no longer resolves (curl 000). download.php is the only download path linked from the homepage, so the product_url is kept (canonical page, server-side error, not moved); warning refreshed with 2026-06-27. Site overall up so activity_status left active.
